reviews.review_text:
Adventure of a lifetime!
It's an enormous unpolished mess of a game that I have nothing, but praises and adoration for.
This is the best CRPG that I've ever experienced, and that includes Baldur's Gate 3, with a writing of pure love and passion for fantasy genre.

This game is absolutely jaw droppingly insane with the customization options, truly a masterclass in DnD style class making. I can't believe the amount of options. I think I got this game on sale for like less than 10 bucks, holy moly. It's baldurs gate 3 for nerds. In the best way. High, high quality game.
its an owlcat game. buy is and wait 1 year or 2 after they release it and you got yourself one hell of a good game.

I loved this game. 100+ hours, and yet I haven't finished my first playthough. I don't know, at some point everything just felt like a chore: prebuffing a lot, having so many summons, combat taking over 3 business days. Story's somewhat interesting but I don't feel that immersed since there's a lack of voice acting.

There was a point in time where I genuinely enjoyed the game, wanted to finish it, and maybe do more playthroughs. But now? I'm just burnt out. Demotivated. I can't anymore. I'm sorry. Maybe I'll give it another try again at some point but definitely not in the near future.

I suggest NOT playing turnbased mode for this game. Sure, it might make combat easier (especially if you come from a game like BG3), but it also makes it way slower. Way, way slower.

What a huge disappointment this game turned out to be. After having a blast with Kingmaker and after taking a break to not burn myself out I jumped into this game. Just like many other sequels that don't live up to the previous title this does the same thing.


[h1]The good:[/h1]

Didn't know I needed Arueshalae in my life, but now that I experience her dialogue, the awesome quest, the funny party banter, the romance and the ending slides I have to say that they knocked it out of the park with this character. I will be holding this developer to this standard moving forward with their newer games.

I loved to play the angel mythic path, loved the interactions I got from the NPCs during the main and side quests.


[h1]The bad:[/h1]

Again with the quality of the companion quests being inconsistent. Just like in the first game some characters get a good story arc while others are begging for scraps, Camellia and Wenduag have bad or non existing personal quests. Literally every other companion has 10/10 personal quests. I can guess why one of the two had such a bad character quest, it was because the writers had to take time to somehow write Jubilost into the story for a short encounter because why not.

Again just like the first game, this one felt a bit too long with too much padding added to the game. The army/crusade parts were boring and somehow a downgrade compared to the kingdom management.

The whole mythic path feels like an after though even with how intertwined the system is with the story.

I hate the fact that they added the possibility of having a mount and locked it behind wasting a skill on animal companion if you even have that skill in your class repertoire.


[h1]The really bad:[/h1]

Chapter 4. Yeah, no wonder why it's hated. The camera gimmick was annoying, the fights were way too frequent and with too many enemies. I wish they put as much effort as Larian did with their redo of act 3 & 4 of Original Sin 2.

Bugs have become synonymous with Owlcat and their lack of interest in patching them after they release the first and second season passes. Maybe in the future we will see a third, fourth and a fifth season pass.
They love to add DLCs to the game, either by putting a new character in the early chapters and forcing you to replay the game or some badly written side stories that go nowhere. More isn't always better, especially when talking about Owlcat and DLCs because they introduce new bugs and never fix existing ones.

I don't hate puzzles, but I do hate badly implemented puzzles and Inevitable Excess delivered only bad puzzles.

At some point the game forces you to play turned based. It was not fun since I played the whole game real time. If you give me the option to choose how to play the game then don't take it away for some stupid arbitrary reason.


[h1]Conclusion:[/h1]

After reading this review you would wonder why I recommend this game. I wouldn't outright say don't buy the game, just buy it on a sale and don't expect too much from it. It's an ok game, but don't expect it to be another BG 3 like people make it out to be.
like with kingmaker, the writing kinda sucks and the combat is frustrating and uninteresting. plus the army/large battle gameplay is also annoying and bad! just play rogue trader it's so much better

The developers couldn't code their way out of a wet paper bag.  The real punch in the face is that you won't know it until you are WELL into the game.  It deteriorates over time, easily ignorable at first, but come Act 4 the game becomes close to unplayable.  And if you google it, you'll find numerous threads about it stretching back years, and they still haven't fixed the issues.

And of course by that point you can't ask for a refund because of how much time you've suck into the game.

Soo, I just now finished act 1, which is like the equivilent of a long intro/extended tutorial to the game that all games have. It is a must buy, especially on sale. I think i just bought it with everything included for 15$ give or take this last sale that just ended or is ending shortly as of this post. Some downsides first 1. Make sure you DO NOT REST/SLEEP in game longer then 3 days at the very start as that will immedietly override important quest triggers in chapter 1 for Mythic quest lines much later in the game. Do those first even if your character's are slow as mules from exaustion (you'll understand and thank me later). 2. This game is a Min/Maxer's & Completionist's dream game, so many different playstyles and ways of doing a single quest. You can of course skip these and make everything custom settings aka easy mode, for the story (worth it as well) turning off/on turn based mode as well. 3. Make sure to do ALL the Mythic option dialogues in chapter 1, it doesn't lock you in to them but enables them to be picked down the road, if you do not pick them at the start in dialogue you shut off the ability to pick those specific mythic classes later on. 4. Some mythic classes are secret, merging from others, so you can start good and holy and then become a terrible POS villain, I wont include specific spoiler's but you can go wild with your aspirations and goals in this game.

It's a very deep and complex RPG with a ton of different ways to play. I'm 80 hours in and I probably have atleast another 80 hours to go. There is a lot of reading but the writing is good. The voice acting isn't bad but could be better. lots of systems to min max and some battles can be extremely challenging. It's not a see the bad guy run and kill him type of game.  A lot of buffing and debuffing is critical on the harder difficulties. The story is pretty good so far. For a game with elves and dwarves and orcs in it, adult themes are there, for sure a fantasy game for adults to enjoy.

Amazing game, amazing story, I love this game so much,

My only advice is the whole army managment is, either play in easy or look for a tutorial. But I love this game, is difficult as ♥♥♥♥ but I love it. Best d20 rpg videogame I ever played, I love desna and I love this game.

Honestly the best owlcat. One of the best crpg as well.

Feature real-time/turn-based combat like dragon age. 
Love the companion. 
The story and plot point is absolute perfection. With replayability so high due to all the twist in the campaign. 
Also contain management for those who would like it
The combat if i remember is kinda shaky.

I love this game. But I really can't recommend it for most players.

People like to pretend there's this deep complex combat system. It's overly complicated and shallow. You'll spend hours reading arcane rules that find stacking +1 bonuses that don't work in-game with the rules as written, and then auto-attack through the game.

The difficulty curve is stupid, and unfortunately, the beginning tutorial dungeon is the worst. You've got no options, and the game is a flat slog of load/reload or turn the difficulty down to joke levels. I've played completed this game multiple times, but 80% of the time the beginning segment of the game reminds me why I put this game down.

But then you make it to the 20 hour mark. The game starts to give you options. The story gets truly epic. The characters are fun. Crazy ♥♥♥♥ is happening.

And then all pacing grinds to a halt because they put a moon-logic puzzle in your way that requires Google or a guide.

I bought this on sale a year or so ago after a friend recommended it to me, and I regret not playing it earlier.

The only games similar I played was a full campaign of BG3 and a little bit through Divinity 2 Original Sin, with the latter not really vibing with, but WOTR really blew my expectations out of the water. I did NOT expect to fully dive and like this game THIS much. 

In the beginning, the choices felt pretty overwhelming like choosing my classes, feats, and spells, because I really only came from a BG3 background. So in ACT 1, I felt like I was just going around blind and choosing whatever seemed good (even if it wasn't.) I may have stopped there, but the story REALLY kept me. All the characters captivated me and I absolutely LOVED how all of my choices mattered. The general story was interesting and kept me wanting to learn more, which is something that really matters to me.

Fast forward, and every day I'm staying up till midnight playing and WANTING to play this over my usual games more and more. I started understanding combat, spells, feats, and how my choices would snowball down the line.

Without spoiling, this story really takes the cake for me. The only other game I put this much time, effort, and huge drive to play every day was Dragon Age: Inquisition waaay back then.

I love all the characters and I'm glad they didn't just hit on me when I started to be nice... I LOVE PURE PLATONIC FRIENDSHIP.

Also, Lann is the best. The only other 2D character I think I fell in love with out of all the otomes and other games I've played. 

TLDR - YES PLAY GAME.

Positives
Absurd amounts of content
Great Writing
Tons of companions with fully fleshed out story lines and party banter. 
Multiple paths for the main quest, tons of choices, tons of ways to play. Story will completely change at points depending on what you do. 
Class building offers nearly endless options


Negatives
Hard as ♥♥♥♥, a blind play through on normal with zero pathfinder experience will repeatedly kick your ass.
Voice acting is used way too sparingly, it's really great in the scenes it is in, I just wish there was more. 
The game is 30 billion nested triggers, occasionally one breaks, save frequently

An improvement in every way upon the already enjoyable Kingmaker.  Owlcat once again shows its ability to tell a great story within the framework of multiple organisational and tactical systems.

The game does ramp up the complexity of choices in regards to character progression, already a little daunting in Kingmaker, but there are many guides and a wealth of help, if one wants, available online.

Still early days for me with this title, but enjoying it immensely so far.

Absolutely loved the game, I chose to play the game as if it was me in the role of the commander and funnily enough ended as a chaotic good Azata. This game has allowed me to learn and grow as a person simply because the options presented allowed me to do what I truly would have done and then being able to see the consequences of my actions. It was a valuable experience and I will definitely be going back to play this game again after my well earned break.

My absolute favorite CRPG. The power level you can achieve truly makes you feel like a god, and the different paths to power that are available help to keep things fresh on new playthroughs. There are some glitches, but nothing game breaking that I've found.

My large hour count in this game is because I like fantasy RPGs and because I typically leave the game running even when I'm not playing it. Having said that, I wouldn't recommend this game (or Pathfinder: Kingmaker) for players that are looking for an epic romp through a fantasy world. The developers of this game seem to sadistically want to punish the player if they don't have forehand knowledge of what each encounter is going to be before they encounter (and there are a lot of encounters) or if you're not a nerd that mix-maxes and spends hours on creating a character/party that will out-nerd all other nerds. This is not a Larian game. If you're not one of those aforementioned nerds who knows the whole story line than you will get killed. Repeatedly. Along with every member of your party. If that's your idea of recreation, then you do you, But for players that are just looking to have an adventure akin to a great fantasy novel, you won't find it here. The games good moments, of which there are many, are overshadowed by the poor design that revolves around endless combat.

One of my absolute favorites. Quite by accident I stumbled through the story in a way that felt like it was written for me. Finished it and played it through again right after. I wish I could play for the first time again.

I love this game.

It's a phenomenal game, I'm just so ass, I swear to you it's not actually 100 hours per playthrough. Story is really nice and it gives you a lot of freedom with your choices. Difficulty options also let you challenge yourself however you want, whether you hate yourself or want it to be piss easy. I'm not a huge fan of crusade mode, but you can toggle it in settings and it also has its own difficulty settings.

I wanted to love this, I really did. I loved both Baldurs Gate titles when they were new, and the remasters. I liked Pillars of Eternity but felt it lacked in many ways. Kingmaker was OK and had some flaws that for me made the game stall and I got tired and quit.

Pathfinder 2 (my title, not official) improves some elements, but it has some of the problems the first one did.
The general dungeon diving is excellent, your characters have plenty of good abilities, theres loads of spells, equipment, and special items. Tons of unique, exciting monsters. And the story is great. Characters are as complex as Baldurs Gate if not more so.

But just like the first game I DESPISE wandering around a mostly empty map and all those ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ nodes with nothing on them. I'd rather just go straight to the locale and see a message "some time has passed". 
Empire management got annoying and tedious very fast and I disabled it, which apparently permanently locks me out of some quests but I was so fed up by then I simply did not care. 

Eventually I got tired of slogging around the map incredibly slowly and quit. Can't recommend this, even though everyone else on earth seems to love it. 

By the way letting you have a triceratops named Bismuth was friggen genius and makes me wonder why more games don't.

One of the best CRPGs I've played, up there with some of the best. The only thing holding this game back is that Owlcat did not have a high enough budget to make this a higher production value game like Baldurs Gate 3.
Love the story & companions, game is a lot of fun.

Of the two Owlcat Pathfinder CRPGs, this is the better one. The companions are better written, the crusade system while it has its flaws is better than the kingdom system, and it has more classes/variety to said classes. [Wish they had backported a bunch of these classes actually]. The writing overall tends to be better as well.

Still wish Halberds had actually made it into the game but can not have everything. Makes me sad though we probably will never see another owlcat pathfinder game
Spent hours staring at the Character Creation screen just theory crafting. TB and RTWP is both amazing to play.

Great if not greatest crpg I've ever played very immersive with the class choices and many more choices littered throughout the game, in depth mechanics that make you think about what to do next and multiple difficulty choices making it friendly for both pros of the system and new comers trying wotr for the first time! Get this damn game and get nerdy!!!!!!!!! ps i have 600 plus hours on the playstation as well

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yshNnrMsofU

Same with Kingmaker, this game would've been better with complete voice acting.

It is a perfect game in so many ways. Really recommend it.

What I DO hold against it is its unforgiveness: if you miss a small detail of a (seemingly insignificant quest) it punishes you, and not right away, but at the end. So you need to spend 100+ hours to find out if you have missed something to have your expected ending. Not cool, OwnCat! I would like a bit more forgiveness or at least some warning regarding the important/key stuff during the game.

Other than this, the game is literally perfect. Keep up the good work.

What I love is the nostalgic feel of the game. It brings me back to a time when games were simpler in their functioning and highly engaging in their storytelling. The game feels plenty rewarding and the abundance of quests helps immerse me in this wonderful world full of exciting surprises. What I did not love include minor things. Mostly cosmetic things, like I really do not like almost any of the character portraits. And I strongly dislike every single one of the physical looks of the characters in the game. The pictures are one thing, but the characters themselves look kind of creepy and surreal. I don't like that. On a scale of 1-10, I give them a solid 5 for the character appearances. Not so bad that I'm turned off from the game but not good enough to make me fall in love with anyone but a RARE few. I also wish you had a difficulty level warning of some kind because some places are indeed much harder than others if you're playing on advanced difficulty thresholds, and with the open world aspect, you are bound to go waltzing in to a punishing area without knowing it. I'm actually back and forth on that option because the surprise is indeed a surprise. Sometimes a warning may be warranted. Additionally, when creating your character, the absolute plethora of class options is a little chaotic for me. I feel like you missed an opportunity to create a much more elaborate level up ladder system that could have felt much more rewarding. (Fighter becomes ... becomes ... becomes ...) But instead, it feels like I have to do some research on every single class option and then map them myself to whatever I want to accomplish...Being new to the game, I don't exactly know what I want and I'd like my options to remain open based on my gameplay. It feels like I don't have that option as much at the get-go because of the class clutter. Don't get me wrong, I love the vast array of possible options, but it very much feels like I better be specific in what I want from the start or I'll miss out on an advanced tier. I hope I am explaining that well enough. It just feels cluttered and confusing. Suffice it to say, there is a whole lot going on with this game, and if you are willing to sit down, take your time, read, and progress patiently then it is a fantastic game! 4/5 stars for me, which is quite good. Highly recommend.

What can I say that hasn't already been said? This crpg giant has the largest build diversity of any game I have ever played, more than that, the builds genuinely feel different to play. Its not like Elden Ring, where you swap from one damage stick to another, and all the spells are samey, not that I don't love that game, but you probably get my point. When you play as a monk, you feel like a monk, and when you play as a wizard, you can feel the power of your spells. Even inside each class, there are a multitude of archetypes for you to choose from. You can multiclass to your hearts content, generally, there are some exceptions, and almost all of the classes feel intentional. Moreover, all of the characters, even the one's that aren't companions, feel like they lived truly complex lives, Irabeth especially has one of my favorite character moments in the entire game. I haven't beat this game yet, as you could probably tell from my rant I love messing around with different builds, so the farthest I have ever gotten is near the beginning of act 4, and my current run just made it to act 3. People who have played PF: WOTR before might notice that I haven't mentioned one of the best aspects of this game, mythic paths, which are added on top of your pre-existing builds. That is because you don't truly see them till act 3, and I have two playthroughs currently at the very beginning of the act, and my one in act 4, which took Trickster, skipped a lot of the fun shenanigans you can do with that path. Thus, I hardly consider myself the person to talk to about that feature that I know to be amazing because I watch a lot of guides. The singular problem that I have with this game is the reliance on dice rolls for basic things, some locations on the map won't be revealed if you don't roll high enough on your perception check, and honestly, save-scumming just feels bad. Also, I didn't really suffer from this, but it can be overwhelming, I recommend playing BG3 and/or DOS 2 beforehand, or just any simpler crpg. 
P.S. Arushalae, Ember, and Nenio are all best girl, and you won't be able to convince me otherwise.
P.S. P.S.  Lann, Regill, and Woljif are all best boy, and you won't be able to convince me otherwise, just don't tell Regill I said anything lol.  
P.S P.S. P.S. Sorry this rant was so long, go buy the game.

Where WOTR really shines is in regards to the mechanics of the classes and abilities. There are a lot of classes to choose from and different ways to build your character and party. 
Some things could be explained more clearly in the game, for example which buffs stacks and which dont. Some things also seem clearly overpowered while others are very bad. For example, a morale boost to attack is best attained by the common spell heroism (and variants of it), but there are other classes that get morale boosts to attack as seperate abilities. These look valuable when you are at the drawing board, but in reality they are often worthless as they give no benefit in conjunction with a spell like heroism.
Another negative is that you have 6 characters and can thus always have a full arcane and a full divine caster. The in-betweeners (e.g. inquisitor) often perform poorly both as martials and casters and since you already have access to divine spells from a full divine caster they just end up as a worse martial character. It would have been nice if these classes had been implemented to be more powerful since they often have nice flavor.
One last negative is that not all things are implemented properly, mounted combat for example is very janky and thus playing a mounted character will look good on paper but work less well in practice. 
The writing in my opinion is okay but not anything spectacular.

All that said the game is a lot of fun to play around with. You will likely however reroll a lot and try different builds and parties before you find something you like. I guess you could also just play at a lower difficulty setting and roll with whatever. It is highly adviced to play the game in turn-based mode for the most part as that it what makes the game actually interesting tactically.
Never imagined a game could soft lock u in 2026. ♥♥♥♥ the crusade morale system. U dont even know u ♥♥♥♥♥♥ up untils hours of game play

Poxa, sim! Todo jogo tem review negativaa, não se apegue a isso! Perfeição não existe, apenas porque o perfeito é subjetivo. Falo aqui, caso deseje ler, algo breve sobre a avaliação positiva, mas para se aprofundar no game e saber se ele vale seu tempo e dinheiro, existem vídeos no YT melhores pra isso. 
Pra Review eu recomendo o vídeo do canal "Me Chamo Indie", foi com esse vídeo que bati o martelo e decidi testar o jogo.

Recém saí do primeiro mapa com 20 horas jogadas sem pressa, e não poderia estar mais satisfeito em achar um RPG CRPG que eu não abandono em 3 horas de gameplay porque é totalmente enfadonho.
Particularmente eu não gosto, mas é um jogo para se terminar mais de uma vez, há várias escolhas de caminhos míticos pra se seguir, caminhos míticos são -conservando informações agora- uma coisa que tem relação com poderes e escolhas. 
É muito agradável a gameplay, a história e o mundo são bastante interessantes e com personagens marcantes, há modos de dificuldades para os mais casuais até os hardcore.

A Campanha principal tem legenda (fã made. Agradeço, verdadeiramente, aos HERÓIS, sem exagero, Heróis! Aos fãs que traduziram o jogo. Também a desenvolvedora que adicionou os de modo "oficial" sem a necessidade de mods. A localização é bastante boa! Você pode encontrar ao acaso um pelo no ovo, mas é um trabalho incrível. ) em PT-BR, Infelizmente as DLCs não estão localizadas em nosso idioma.

Played this game a few years ago on normal and it was pretty enjoyable, trying to play again on core which is apparently the "intended" experience and this is the most abysmal ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ I have ever played in my entire life, the shield maze is impossible. How a game can have such great writing and such awful, disgusting combat design should really be studied for years to come. Owlcat devs need to go the ♥♥♥♥ outside or something.

I really want to like this game. I'm a fan of Pathfinder and first edition but I stopped counting natural 1s,2s and 3s after 50! Apparently you're just not supposed to hit with two-handed martial classes. Also, stinking cloud bomb over and over at 3rd level just isn't fun.
Mostly solid mechanics, engaging story, player choices give good replay value.
The gameplay is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ terrible. Anyway, best game since Dark Messiah of Might and Magic. If you know, you know.

This game has one of the best stories I have ever experienced, wrapped in some of the clunkiest, most awkward mechanics I have ever seen in a modern video game. First of all, you need to approach this game like EUIV, not Skyrim. This game is fundamentally unintuitive, lacks the information necessary to understand it, and cannot be fully learned by just playing it. Its mechanics are unforgiving and if you do anything wrong, you are guaranteed to lose. Quality of life features are almost completely absent, and in terms of lessons learned by other cRPGs, this one ignores all of them. You will suffer playing this game, and not in the fun "challenge" kind of way, but in a Sisyphus war of attrition. I think about uninstalling the game every single time I launch it. 
However, it is just so atmospheric, full of awesome characters, and fundamentally fascinating that I keep playing it nonetheless. For 6€ on sale right now, it's a great deal.

I hate how this game gets me immersed and attached with its high quality story and literature, but then forces me to quit because of its bugs. I was near the end of Chapter 2 when suddenly I could not open anything with a lock, whether it's a door, a chest, or a trap. I did not use any mods so it's just the game itself's issue. Unable to fix it, I have to reluctantly give up playing the game. It's really heartbreaking.

If you like Baldur's Gate 1&2, this is a no-brainer purchase. Yeah, BG3 has its flair and cinematic dialog, but this game has the 'real-time with pause' attacking like the old games or turn-based if you want. The story choices are as in-depth as you would expect from any memorable cRPG, and Owlcat has perfected this. Hug your friends, romance them, or...eat them? If you're not sold by now, look at the average playtime for the reviews, you're gonna get your money's worth, I'm telling you.

Ojala hubiera una opción intermedia entre recomendar y no. Es un muy buen CRPG, tiene muchas cosas que te atrapan, pero por dios está diseñado con las patas. 30% de tu tiempo se pierde en ver a tus personajes ir de aca para allá en el mapa mundial , en la mazmorra. o esperando en pantallas de carga. Hay encuentros que requieren cosas MUY especificas para lidiar con ellos. Hay opciones de dialogo que no te dicen del todo las consecuencias de elegirlas y me bloquee de un par de cosas de historia por eso,

Por otro lado por dios hay cosas que son un slog fest tremendo, porque hay enemigos a la vuelta de cada esquina todos con 40 AC y resistencia de hechizos? De que me sirve tener hechizos de alto nivel si todos los enemigos son INMUNES a ellos?????  La historia de los compañeros está buena, algunos tienen mejor que otras, lo suficiente para atraparte pero no para tirarles flores a los escritores. 

Lo mejor que tiene este juego es su versatilidad para armar tu personaje y la cantidad de HORAS que le podes meter a este juego. Pero si todos los enemigos son 90% Demonios cómo que no sirven builds contra por ejemplo animales. 

Para mi es un 7 muy solido este juego, me encantan varios npcs y es a la par del Kingmaker pero no me deja un buen sabor cómo éste me dió en su momento.


TLDR:  7/10 jugalo si sos vicio y te encanta sufrir

Game is incredible, but please add polish language, there is many polish people that would play this game, but so many text in not native language is not so easy too understand everything if someone speak A2-B1 language level. There is many Pole that play RPG's. Many Pole played this game in english as well, but would more if we have polish translation.

I really enjoy making builds in this game. Yes, i am not the "build" player, more role-play players, but it is fun, when i can do characters from my book in the game, even if my world is very specific. Dark melancholic bard with ice and air spells? You can build this. Pink, crazy, sweet shaman girl from strong north with ice spells and play drums to fight with sadness and also she have big wolf?  You can build this.

Only one thing: bards and skalds don't have playing or dancing animations. All other things are really good. Very complex system, but if someone want to do easy build can do this also. I am not into pen and paper Pathfinder, but more Call of Cthulhu 7e player, but still i realy enjoy with all the builds you can get in Pathfinder, so... maybe some day i will try pen and paper Pathfinder.
I hate this game passionately when I'm playing it, but when I'm not playing it I'm very sad and miss it so much.

Made a silly dwarf, 200+ hours later i ended the game as a member of a succubus queen's harem for eternity.

No regrets.
This game ruined other RPGs for me and made me an Owlcat fangirl. Easily one of the best CRPGS if not the best RPG I played.

Good game, many hours of fun, soundtrack is servicable, load times minimal, bugs almost non-existant, difficulty adjustable to your heart's desire. If you played this game's prequel, this is even better. if you played Baldur's Gate 3, this is a more complex, harder to get version of that. If you liked either you will likely like this as well.
8/10, would recommend.

The best game of all times, my personal favorite (I love Pathfinder 1st Edition <3 ).
I really hope they make another AP into a videogame, I would play the heck out of it :)

I find this game interesting, fun and challenging. I do get frustrated by the lack of clarity as to what is going on, and how to navigate maps, and complete quests efficiently. However it is fairly old by now, so there is lots of helpful info out there. Above all, is the variety of builds , and an interesting world to experience.

This is certainly not a bad game and I see why people like it, but i want to post this review just so people have more of a guide with how it lines up with specific tastes. Because for me personally, I've always been a turn based tactics guy when it comes to CRPG's, I could never get into into RTWP combat despite many attempts. Now although this game does technically have both, I feel like even the turn based mode is RTWP at heart. So yeah, if you're like me and can't stand RTWP combat probably not for you. But for people who like/love RTWP combat, you'll probably love it, even if you don't like turn based tactics.

I sunk more than 200 hours into a single playthrough of this game. (The campaign is that long.) In the end, I was left unsatisfied, despite the good writing and memorable characters.

Like any CRPG that isn’t Disco Elysium, the core gameplay is tactical combat. I stuck to normal difficulty and found most encounters tough but fair after figuring out the game’s systems. (It was around hour 40 that I found the “Spellbook” screen that let my mage use more than a couple of spells a day.) My biggest gripe is the difficulty spikes: A lot of your spells and abilities are useless against the toughest enemies, and their high AC means that your attacks will struggle to even hit them. I found that my bow-wielders were far more effective in terms of accuracy and damage output than my sword-wielders, which is one of many puzzling balance issues in the game.

Speaking of puzzles: Ugh. Just skip the puzzles.

And then there are the bugs. Sometimes the game crashed. Sometimes a quest condition failed to trigger. But for me, the most frustrating part was when I’d take an action in combat, and… nothing happened. The log would say “Nenio cast a spell,” and Nenio’s turn would end, but the spell wouldn’t go into effect. Did a roll fail? Was a condition not met? Who knows! It felt like the game just randomly decided to ignore my commands.

Despite some good qualities, the game just had too many issues to recommend it.

At the time of this review I have not finished the game in its entirety. I have finished everything leading up to act 5, including DLC, and have finished the majority of content prior to the final act 6.

Why I write this now rather than wait 20 more hours is because the content I am currently doing "The Enigma" is infamous, but frankly should be more known by everyone going into this game. Many people hate The Enigma and say its the one bad part of the game. In my eyes this is untrue, it is the culmination of the game, it is everything wrong with it and that so many act like it came out of nowhere is baffling.

The game does not respect your time, because the writers did not respect their own time. There is no symbolism, there is no deeper meaning, there is no grand message or vision that was driving the creation of this game, it was money and any writing that indicates otherwise was created purely from the stance of "how do we make it seem like we (the creators of the game) care." As I played the game I went back and forth on my belief of this, mostly thinking they had a group of incompetent writers with maybe 1-2 people trying to steer the ship in the right direction. They have an overarching message of "is it better to be born good, or to overcome your inner evil" and that "anyone can be good" which is done sloppily all across the story with no deeper interpretation of these ideas than presented here. I wanted to believe that while trite, this message still had some vision to it. Perhaps one of the writers had a scene in particular they dreamed up that fueled them to want to make this. This is not the case, and you can see it in The Enigma

The Enigma is your classic puzzle palooza. The challenge gauntlet in your RPG. Its themed around a demon lord of mysteries, knowledge and of course enigmas, except the theming is absolute ass. There attempts to be a motif of philosophy, knowing the meaning of life, of true knowledge. I say attempt, because zero effort was put into this. You get grade school babies first riddles in the place of any sort of genuine pondering. We are talking "What is full of holes but still holds water?" (a sponge) level of questions here, just genuinely insulting all around. With the turd shaped bow on top being that throughout the dungeon you are asked questions about the meaning of life, love, relationships, etc. All of which the intended answer being "Nothing" 

Like I'm not expecting them to put the highest degree of effort into this optional content, ya know? And they can have a wet cardboard excuse of "its an evil demon lord trying to entrap people with its intentionally bad/malicious ideas" if they want, but it not even the bare minimum, it was made with zero love and only exists to pad out a game, which in itself only exists to pad out their wallets.

Pros: Interesting story, roleplay, enemies, deep character customization
Cons: Boring fights and exploring location. 

Ok with fights, I got used to it. But I tired to came across monsters after every fight in 10 steps. I think this game should only be played on easy mode so that the battles don't distract too much from the story

Despite finding it frustrating at times, I really enjoyed the overall experience .It's a game that allows players to be powerful and avoids making you feel like a glorified errand boy, which owlcat games are good at in general.

There are of course a lot of flaws with the game, especially with how dragged out a lot of the mega dungeons are, as well as the unintuitive puzzle design. It sometimes feels like a game that should be much older than it is with how inconvenient and tedious things can end up being.

Despite all that, It's still one of my favourite crpg experiences. I enjoyed all of the companions, and while the story was for the most part standard fare, I don't think it ever needed to be anything more than that. It serves as a larger than life saga written in myth, and that is a big reason the power fantasy of the game works so well . And the mythic paths gives so many options for roleplaying flavour 

I know the pathfinder system itself can be overwhelming, but playing on normal difficulty it felt more than manageable. By act 5 most encounters felt trivially easy.           
     
Overall I'd give it a 9/10. It won't be everyone's cup of tea, but to me it scratched all the right parts of my brain.

Great CRPG with a lot of replay value. Only real negatives are that the companions are pretty hit or miss and that it's bit woke. Found only a few minor bugs in 400h. Strongly recommend using mostly turn based mode.

Loved this game until they introduce the "crusade" part where it turns from being amazing CRPG into utter trash strategy game which actively blocks you from progressing the game. This single mechanic is so anti fun it has undone all the fun I had with this game. Uninstalled.
I am liking it so far. The story sounds good and the play is interesting

Literally just giving it a negative review because the auto-save function doesnt activate during maps and sometimes the game will, 3 hours into a big map just spawn an essentially unkillable monster that you cannot run away from because the game doesn't have an escape mechanic.

throwing 50 enemies at you at lvl 3 is some of the laziest game design ever. having to turn down the difficulty shouldnt be the strat needed to be a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ poorly designed interaction. this feel like the same level of ♥♥♥♥♥♥ stupid ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ oblivion tried to pull and damn near ruined elder scrolls
The graphics are a bit dated but the story is engaging, there is a lot of reading though

Epic. That about sums it. Nearly 500 hrs to complete the vast arc from Golarion to the Abyss and then back again. Maybe this was due to doing everything turn based. Note this is optional but needed for the harder enemies. Enjoyed the companion quests, tried to complete everything but still left several missions undone due to obscure pathways and unintended sequencing. The whole time I was always catching up to demon strengths and would regularly get poleaxed out of the blue. A couple of balancing issues in the main story line but in general a well thought out campaign, freedom to play your morality compass, and a strategic layer (Crusade Mode) to keep it interesting.  

What’s to like: Top CPRG. Good art direction, solid graphics, fitting music composition rolled into a grand adventure to fix the world. I can see a lot of inspiration for Baldurs Gate 3 here. If you like narrative and lore, this game is for you.

What’s not quite right: Some balancing issues with a several fights. Took me about 3 days to deal with 2 Gallu Stormcallers prior to the penultimate demon. Time wasted on screen loading leaving Dresden and re-entering between Crusade mode.  Companions that would disappear due to morality choices I guess – looking at you Regill.

Worth a Play: Yes

I do not think the juice is worth the squeeze on this one. Lot of minor issues, very buggy for a crpg in my experience, and the combat is very restrictive with a lot of dead choices. Complex without the rewards for being complex imo, but that's a 1st edition pathfinder thing. Dialogue writing feels like i'm being talked at in most conversations and the game doesn't offer much in the way of informing your character's personality or history, it feels like any agency is fitting into the alignment system rather than offering real responses.
Never liked Pathfinder's core system, but Kingmaker was fun. Story and characters here are just meh.

This is indeed a mess of a game and should not be played by anyone looking to have fun. It's a worthless slog with poorly written clues to vague ideas that require looking at a walk through to even get a hint of what you are missing. There are way too many useless interactions with meaningless NPC. The world building is terrible and the game can't decide if it's railroading you or making the puzzles so needlessly difficult that they aren't even worth the time to solve them. There is really no payoff to anything. Every quest is just an excuse to do the same boring wandering without real aim. It would require a ton of mods just to make it a decent game and it's just not worth it at all.
VG. less plodding than kingmaker and gives far more UI information. Massive upgrade as far as i can tell
RUBBISH. The visual halo of Aasimar usually disappears somehow, and ruined the whole game.
Turn on turn based mode and play it like it's DnD on a computer and you'll love it, as long as you know how to press F5 & F8!
I like the genre, and the character building best

Ставлю минус за ужасный геймдизайн. Он сделан из рук вон плохо. Я проведу параллели с NWN2, так как они довольно похожи и я наиграл много часов в обеих играх. 
1. Первые несколько уровней в системе ДНД - неиграбельные. Слишком мало хп, слишком большой урон. Поэтому в НВН первые уровни - обучение. В случае, если игрок не желает долбиться в ворота головой - он может пропустить обучения и перейти непосредственно к сюжету, который начинается с 3-го уровня. Это значит игрок сразу может поглядеть на персонажа в частично сформированном виде. В пасфайндере разработчики положили большой болт на комфортный старт - уже на втором уровне вы будете сражаться с врагами, которые за один удачный удар отправляют вашего танка к Харону, когда у тенка 22 хп, а один удар критом у противников - 25. Если кратко - онбординга в игре нет. 
2. Если в большинстве подобных игр игрока сталкивают с разными существами, то здесь почти все противники - демоны. Которые имеют кучу резистов и уменьшений урона. В итоге все начальные уровни  бОльшая часть группы просто бесполезно стоит - лучники наносят по 1-2 урона (если не догадаться купить стрелы из холодного железа), маги просто смотрят, так как ячеек очень мало, а заклинания нулевого уровня просто бесполезны против демонов. 
3. Ужасное расположение противников. У вас группа 3-го уровня, вы заходите в случайный подвал и сталкиваетесь с демоном, у которого иммунитет к физурону. Поздравляю - ваша группа умирает, потому что ячеек у магов не хватит на то, чтобы убить его, лучники и милишники наносят по 0 урона. 
4. Крысы. За это разработчикам уготован отдельный котёл в аду. Потому что группа, у которой ещё нет слотов для лечения болезней сталкивается с этими тварями прямо в начале, на третьем уровне группы. И даже если каким-то чудом группа выйдет победителями - они будут носителями такого букета болезней, что в современный медцентр их и на порог не пустили бы, а сразу укатали в саркофаги и захоронили как ядерные отходы.
5. Классы. Как же много классов и архетипов добавили разработчики! Насколько их много - на столько же они и бесполезны. Вы создаёте персонажа, проходите с ним всю свистопляску первых уровней просто чтобы поглядеть, что он бесполезен. Что демоны вертели на разных местах его фиты и перки и первые 5-7 уровней он просто НЕИГРАБЕЛЕН. И ты с ужасом понимаешь, что тебе нужно снова проходить 1-2 уровни чтобы попробовать что-то другое. Нам дали кучу архетипов, которые отличаются 1-2 фитами и вроде должны перевернуть геймлей. Но нет, по факту это жонглирование яйцами. Если ты выбирал класс в НВН - он реально игрался и ощущался по другому, у него были другие механики, другие возможности.
6. Нет никакого калькулятора фитов/билдера персонажа. Вроде как можно поставить мод и там создать персонажа большого уровня и покрутить его... но это даже не смешно. Я купил игру и хочу играть в неё, а не лазить по форумам и подбирать моды, чтобы выполнить базовые вещи.
7. Подсказки... видно, что разработчики старались, но получилось всё равно плохо. Если в НВН наведя на фит можно было прочитать про него, про все его зависимости, все механики, которые влияют на него, то в пасфайндере есть описание скила. И оно настолько куцое, что даже зная базовые ДНД правила не понятно как он работает и что на него влияет. Тем же, кто не знаком с ДНД... ну я не знаю как они играют.

Мои претензии адресованы исключительно к геймдизайну. В остальном игра неплоха, в неё хочется играть, в ней есть сюжет. Но как же сложно бороться с нежеланием самой игры, чтобы в неё играли.

Wrath of the Righteous is a strategist's dream.  SO many character creation options.  If you get paralyzed by choice, you've been warned.  If you like to obsess over the countless details that can make a build great, you've found your game.  It isn't as intricately built as BG3, and it's certainly not as horny, but the story and characters are interesting.  10/10.

I really want to like this game. In some ways they clearly learned from Kingmaker and actually made a somewhat enjoyable game. I like the writing, I love Pathfinder, this should be a no brainer... But it's just TOO broken to recommend. About the fiftieth time you lose a high level spell or whole turn to the game's ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ during a hard fight, you just kinda get sick of it. This wouldn't be a big deal if the game were easy, but the game faithfully recreates Pathfinder with a level of detail that most GMs wouldn't do for fear of their players getting up and walking out on them. So when a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ glitch is what makes you miss the window for Breath of Life and have to spend a rare resurrection item and THEN go spend a restoration scroll or six or savescum, it really starts wearing thin.

If you can get past the bugs and enjoy being kicked in the ♥♥♥♥ by Owlcat's encounter design, then its a solid game. But that's a hell of an if.
Tried to pick up this game multiple times. The tedious amounts of encounters and enemies you have to kill, as well as the the poor balancing kill the game for me.
The game has been great so far. I hope it gets better once I've left the character creation screen.

I was really on the fence regarding this one. Tried it on PS5 and didn't really get into it. Gave it another shot and jeez, this might be one of the best gaming experiences of my life so far. Not sure how it ends. But 50 hours in and I'm having a blast.

I like this game a lot. But the difficulty jump after Drezen is just too much.
